name: Japan
tagline: Cherry blossoms, temples, and timing that matters more than you think.

majorDisruptions.name: Golden Week
majorDisruptions.note: Peak domestic travel. Most museums and smaller shops close. Flights 40%+ above seasonal average.

majorDisruptions.name: Obon
majorDisruptions.note: Major ancestral observance. Shinkansen and domestic flights heavily booked.

majorDisruptions.name: Shōgatsu (New Year)
majorDisruptions.note: Many businesses close for several days. Quiet but limited.

weather.summary: Four distinct seasons. Spring and autumn offer the most reliable weather.

highlights: Cherry blossom peak shifts by latitude — Tokyo late March, Hokkaido early May.
Autumn foliage runs early November (north) to early December (Kyoto).
Post-Golden Week (mid-May) is the best-value shoulder window.

name: Portugal
tagline: Sun, surf, and shoulder seasons that shame the rest of Europe.

majorDisruptions.name: August holiday closures
majorDisruptions.note: Many family restaurants and small businesses close for the month.

majorDisruptions.name: Semana Santa (Holy Week)
majorDisruptions.note: Processions across the country. Lodging spikes in major cities.

weather.summary: Mild Mediterranean climate. Atlantic coast stays cooler than Spain.

highlights: Shoulder months (Apr–May, Sep–Oct) offer warm weather without peak crowds.
Algarve coast peaks in July–August with 2–3× accommodation prices.
Lisbon and Porto are pleasant year-round outside winter rain.

name: Italy
tagline: Europe's most-loved country. Timing is everything.

majorDisruptions.name: Ferragosto
majorDisruptions.note: National holiday. Cities empty out but coastal resorts are maxed.

majorDisruptions.name: August closures
majorDisruptions.note: Many family-run restaurants and shops close for the month.

majorDisruptions.name: Christmas & Epiphany
majorDisruptions.note: Rome and Vatican crowded; many businesses on reduced hours.

weather.summary: Mediterranean. Hot dry summers, mild wet winters. Alpine regions differ.

highlights: September is arguably the best all-round month — warm, low crowds, harvest season.
Venice acqua alta peaks in Nov–Dec; avoid if bringing older travelers.
Cinque Terre and Amalfi are overwhelming in July–August.

name: Thailand
tagline: Tropical rhythms, massive seasonal swings, cheap when nobody else goes.

majorDisruptions.name: Songkran (Thai New Year)
majorDisruptions.note: Nationwide water festival. Bangkok partially shuts down. Flights expensive.

majorDisruptions.name: Chinese New Year surge
majorDisruptions.note: Enormous inbound tourism from China. Phuket and Bangkok packed.

weather.summary: Tropical monsoon. Dry season Nov–Mar is the natural peak.

highlights: Nov–Feb is perfect weather but also peak western tourism.
Late Apr – early Jun is hot and dry-ish with cheap prices and thin crowds.
Southern islands split: Gulf side vs Andaman side have opposite wet seasons.

name: Greece

majorDisruptions.name: Orthodox Easter
majorDisruptions.note: Major domestic travel. Ferries fill up, islands festive.

majorDisruptions.name: August peak
majorDisruptions.note: Domestic and international peak overlap. Prices 2×, ferries packed.

weather.summary: Mediterranean. Hot dry summers, mild winters on islands.

highlights: Late May and September are ideal — warm sea, bearable crowds.
Santorini is overwhelming in July–August; visit Paros or Naxos instead.
Athens is pleasant Apr–May and Oct–Nov, brutal in July–August.

name: Spain
tagline: Regional calendars create hidden windows — if you know them.

majorDisruptions.name: Semana Santa (Holy Week)
majorDisruptions.note: Seville, Malaga and Andalusia packed with processions and tourists.

majorDisruptions.name: La Tomatina
majorDisruptions.note: Tiny Buñol, but spikes Valencia-region lodging.

weather.summary: Mediterranean on the coast, continental inland. Scorching in July–August.

highlights: Andalusia is brutal in August — 40°C+ common.
September is the best all-round month nationwide.
Regional holidays vary by autonomous community — check Catalonia vs Madrid.

name: Croatia

majorDisruptions.name: August peak
majorDisruptions.note: Peak for Central European travelers. Dubrovnik and Split saturated.

weather.summary: Mediterranean on the coast, continental inland.

highlights: Late May has warm sea, open restaurants, and few cruise ships.
Plitvice is ideal in May and October — pleasant weather, vibrant colors.
Hvar peaks hard in July–August; Korčula and Vis stay calmer.

name: Morocco
tagline: Markets, deserts, mountains — wildly different by season.

majorDisruptions.name: Ramadan
majorDisruptions.note: Many restaurants closed during daylight. Evening souks more lively.

majorDisruptions.name: Eid al-Fitr
majorDisruptions.note: National holiday. Some businesses closed, transport busy.

weather.summary: Vast range. Sahara brutal in summer; Atlas mountains snowy in winter.

highlights: March–April is peak for desert trekking and Marrakech medina weather.
July–August is oppressive in Marrakech — avoid unless heading to Atlas.
Check Ramadan dates: impacts restaurant hours significantly.

name: Vietnam
tagline: North, center, south — three different climates, one country.

majorDisruptions.name: Tết (Lunar New Year)
majorDisruptions.note: Biggest national holiday. Many businesses closed for a week. Transport fully booked.

weather.summary: Tropical. North has distinct seasons; south is wet/dry only.

highlights: Avoid Tết if you want open restaurants and cheap flights.
November is ideal for most of the country.
Halong Bay best in Oct–Dec; Mekong delta best in Dec–Apr.

name: Iceland
tagline: Volcanoes, aurora, and wildly different seasons.

majorDisruptions.name: Christmas / New Year
majorDisruptions.note: Reykjavik packed. Prices spike. Aurora season is peak.

weather.summary: Subpolar oceanic. Summer daylight 20+ hours; winter 4 hours.

highlights: Aurora season is Sep–Apr; peak probability in Oct–Feb.
Midnight sun in June – stunning but crowded.
Interior highland F-roads open only Jun–Sep.
